name: High Calorie Day
description: Daily calorie intake exceeds 120% of target
recommendation: Try to reduce portion sizes or swap high-calorie items for lighter alternatives.

name: Calorie Binge Pattern
description: Exceeded 120% calorie target for 3+ consecutive days
recommendation: Consider adding more vegetables and lean proteins to feel full on fewer calories. Track portions carefully.

name: Excess Sugar
description: Daily sugar intake exceeds 50g (WHO recommends <25g free sugar)
recommendation: Cut back on sweetened beverages and processed snacks. Try fruit as a natural sweet alternative.

name: Diabetic Sugar Spike
description: Single meal contains >30g sugar for users with diabetes
recommendation: This meal has very high sugar content which can cause blood sugar spikes. Consider swapping sugary items for low-GI alternatives.

name: High Sodium
recommendation: Reduce processed foods, canned soups, and salty snacks. Use herbs and spices instead of salt for flavoring.

name: Low Fiber
description: Daily fiber intake below 25g for 3+ days
recommendation: Add more leafy greens, legumes, and whole grains to your meals. Try switching to whole wheat bread and brown rice.

name: Protein Deficit
description: Below 80% of protein target for 5+ days
recommendation: Include more lean meats, eggs, Greek yogurt, beans, or tofu in your meals to meet your protein needs.

name: Meal Skipping - Breakfast
recommendation: Eating breakfast helps kickstart your metabolism and provides energy for the morning. Try quick options like overnight oats or yogurt parfaits.

name: Late Night Eating
recommendation: Eating late can affect sleep quality and digestion. Try to have your last meal at least 2-3 hours before bedtime.

name: Under-eating
recommendation: Eating too little can slow your metabolism and deprive your body of essential nutrients. Make sure you're fueling adequately.
